channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Israel launches new airstrikes on Lebanon as leaders draw closer to a ceasefire with Hezbollah
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Israel's military launched airstrikes across Lebanon on Monday, unleashing explosions throughout the country and killing at least 12 while Israeli leaders appeared to be closing in on a negotiated ceasefire with the Hezbollah militant group.
item.dc:creator: SALLY ABOU ALJOUD, JOE FEDERMAN and SAM METZ, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Special counsel moves to abandon election interference and classified documents cases against Trump
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Special counsel Jack Smith moved to abandon two criminal cases against Donald Trump on Monday, acknowledging that Trump’s return to the White House will preclude attempts to federally prosecute him for retaining classified documents or trying to overturn his 2020 election defeat.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, ERIC TUCKER and CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Eggs are available -- but pricier -- as the holiday baking season begins
item.description: Egg prices are on the rise again as a lingering outbreak of bird flu coincides with high demand during the holiday baking season.
item.dc:creator: Dee-Ann Durbin, Josh Funk And Mark Vancleave, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Supreme Court won’t hear challenge to graphic cigarette warning labels
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court won’t hear a challenge to a federal requirement that cigarette packages and advertising include graphic images demonstrating the effects of smoking. The high court declined to hear the case in a brief written order handed down Monday. Tobacco company R.J.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: First right whales of season gorge on critical food off Massachusetts, giving hope for a strong year
item.description: Scientists who study a critically endangered species of whale that lives off New England said encouraging early signs suggest the animals could have a strong season for feeding and breeding.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Time running out for Senate to pass Social Security Fairness Act
item.description: The House recently passed a Social Security bill but the Senate is running out of time to get it over the finish line.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Drone team helps missing hunter safely escape woods
item.description: PESHTIGO, Wis. (WFRV) - Authorities in Marinette County, Wisconsin, say a missing hunter safely escaped from the woods thanks to help from the sheriff's office's drone team. According to a news release from the Marinette County Sheriff's Office, deputies responded at 5:03 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Nick Witwer, NewsNation

item.title: Former officers in Tyre Nichols case back in court
item.description: Three of the five former Memphis Police Officers charged in Tyre Nichols death are expected to face a judge.
item.dc:creator: Deja Davis, NewsNation

item.title: Father of Maui woman who vanished in Los Angeles found dead near LAX
item.description: Ryan Kobayashi, father of a 31-year-old Maui woman who vanished in Los Angeles nearly two weeks ago, was found dead near Los Angeles International Airport early Sunday morning, officials confirmed.
item.dc:creator: Lily Dallow, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: What's behind the doors of this Missouri cave? A former roller rink, concert stage and more
item.description: Adventure awaits at every turn of one captivating cave in suburban St. Louis, now modernized into a quirky and nostalgic getaway with a storied legacy.
item.dc:creator: Joey Schneider, ABC 4

item.title: Donald Trump Jr., Elon Musk joke about purchasing MSNBC
item.description: Musk’s exchange with Trump Jr. is similar to one he had with a Twitter user in 2017 after he was encouraged to purchase the social media platform.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, ABC 4

item.title: Buccaneers QB Baker Mayfield seeks $12M in lawsuit against his father's company
item.description: An ongoing dispute between Tampa Bay Buccaneers quarterback Baker Mayfield and his family in Austin has boiled over into a lawsuit.
item.dc:creator: Billy Gates, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: New Zealanders save more than 30 stranded whales by lifting them on sheets
item.description: WELLINGTON, New Zealand (AP) — More than 30 pilot whales that stranded themselves on a beach in New Zealand were safely returned to the ocean after conservation workers and residents helped to refloat them by lifting them on sheets.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Should sex abuse evidence set the Menendez brothers free? A judge will decide
item.description: Aunts of Erik and Lyle Menendez testified on their behalf as the brothers seek to have their 1989 convictions reexamined in the shotgun murders of their parents in their Beverly Hills home more than 30 years ago.
item.dc:creator: Jaimie Ding, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Walmart executive says some prices might rise if Trump's tariffs take effect
item.description: A Walmart executive warned that President-elect Trump’s plans to raise tariffs on imports could drive the retail giant to increase the price of store items.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Hezbollah fires about 250 rockets and other projectiles into Israel in heaviest barrage in weeks
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Hezbollah fired about 250 rockets and other projectiles into Israel on Sunday, wounding seven people in one of the militant group's heaviest barrages in months, in response to deadly Israeli strikes in Beirut while negotiators pressed on with cease-fire efforts to halt the all-out war.
item.dc:creator: KAREEM CHEHAYEB and TIA GOLDENBERG, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Art exhibit at Tennessee university museum draws controversy
item.description: The artist said the piece is a response to fascism, which features a swastika morphing into a cross behind
item.dc:creator: Kenzie Hagood, NewsNation

item.title: Remains of Pennsylvania teen at center of 1973 cold case identified as investigation continues
item.description: Ruth Elizabeth Brenneman was last seen leaving for school more than 50 years ago. Now, her remains have been identified as the investigation into her disappearance continues.
item.dc:creator: George Stockburger, Fox 5

item.title: Why 3 ski resorts ban snowboarding in the US
item.description: Utah is home to two of the three ski-only resorts in North America and the reasoning behind the exclusivity differs for each one.
item.dc:creator: Aubree B. Jennings, NewsNation

item.title: Salad topping sold in 22 states recalled over wrong ingredient
item.description: The packagers should contain tortilla strips but instead, consumers have found crispy onion strips inside.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Langenfeld, KRON4

item.title: Feds mum on how Laken Riley's killer got one-way plane ticket from migrant shelter ground zero
item.description: The FBI and ICE declined to provide more information about a "humanitarian flight" that Laken Riley's killer, Jose Ibarra, was granted from New York City to Atlanta in 2023.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fred Harris, former US senator from Oklahoma and presidential hopeful, dies at 94
item.description: Fred Harris, a former U.S. senator from Oklahoma, presidential hopeful and populist who championed Democratic Party reforms in the turbulent 1960s, died Saturday. He was 94. Harris’ wife, Margaret Elliston, confirmed his death to The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: RIO YAMAT, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Man humiliated on Southwest flight wants an apology from airline
item.description: A Southwest email responding to John Kerrigan's complaint uses phrases such as "we regret your disappointment," but the word "sorry" is nowhere to be found.
item.dc:creator: Chris Horne, KTLA 5

item.title: Jason Kelce's wife, Kylie, announces she is pregnant with the couple's fourth child
item.description: Former Philadelphia Eagles center Jason Kelce's wife announced Friday that she's pregnant with the couple's fourth child.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Glen Powell to star in latest hit: a look-alike competition
item.description: After an appearance in "Top Gun: Maverick" and leading roles in "Anyone but You," "Hitman" and "Twisters," Austin native and Hollywood A-lister Glen Powell is set to star in his latest smash hit: A look-alike competition.
item.dc:creator: Kelsey Thompson, Fox 5

item.title: Here are the most heated Thanksgiving food fights
item.description: Thanksgiving dinner may be one of the most anticipated meals of the year for many people, but it is also one of the most debated. From the correct way to prepare the turkey to the best side dishes and desserts, here are the dishes worth fighting over this Thanksgiving.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump taps NFL player Scott Turner as head of HUD
item.description: Turner serves as the chair of the Center for Education Opportunity at America First Policy Institute, a Trump-aligned think tank.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, Fox 5

item.title: NATO head and Trump meet in Florida for talks on global security
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — U.S. President-elect Donald Trump and the head of NATO have met for talks on global security, the military alliance said Saturday. In a brief statement, NATO said Trump and its secretary general, Mark Rutte, met on Friday in Palm Beach, Florida.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Musk, Ramaswamy ‘DOGE’ confidence in Supreme Court may be tested
item.description: Musk and Ramaswamy will head a nongovernmental commission to dismantle government bureaucracy and cut costs.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Historically bad year for dolphin strandings on Cape Cod has scientists looking for answers
item.description: An unprecedentedly bad year for beached dolphins on Cape Cod might have to do with warming waters changing the availability of the animals' food, said scientists hoping to curb the strandings.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Attorneys want the US Supreme Court to say Mississippi's felony voting ban is cruel and unusual
item.description: JACKSON, Miss. (AP) — The U.S. Supreme Court should overturn Mississippi's Jim Crow-era practice of removing voting rights from people convicted of certain felonies, including nonviolent crimes such as forgery and timber theft, attorneys say in new court papers.
item.dc:creator: EMILY WAGSTER PETTUS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Illinois Supreme Court reverses actor Jussie Smollett's conviction
item.description: The Illinois Supreme Court has reversed the conviction of actor Jussie Smollett, who was found guilty of falsely reporting a 2019 hate crime, after he argued that the State's second prosecution was a due process violation.
item.dc:creator: Angelica Sanchez, WFLA, NBC
